games.title: Scourge Survivors
games.tagline: Drop into the breach. Get swarmed. Burn your way deeper until it's over.
games.overview: You are a lone Pyre operator dropped into a breach with one job: survive the Scourge. The horde never stops growing, so neither can you. Auto-scaling weapons, XP, and level-up drafts meet DOOM-fast gunfeel and gore.

features.title: Survive, then go deeper
features.desc: Drop in, get swarmed, grind the Scourge, level up, draft upgrades, and push room to room until you're overrun.

features.title: The loop IS the game
features.desc: No side-mode filler. The Vampire-Survivors core — orbit, bolt, nova — wears DOOM's skin and never lets up.

features.title: Breach-arena maps
features.desc: Every chamber of the breach is its own arena of rooms and levels, with the deepest reaches of Perdition waiting below.

games.title: Deadlane
games.tagline: Hold the lane. The breach is open and the Scourge is already coming.
games.overview: You are the Wardens, and the line is yours to hold. The Scourge floods every chokepoint in numbers no charge could ever clear — so you build, you wall, and you bleed the horde dry. Every lane held is a generation bought.

features.title: Hold the chokepoint
features.desc: Each level is a named lane. Lay down Warden tech, break the swarm's density, and make the breach pay for every step.

features.title: Draft and synergize
features.desc: Wave pressure earns scrap, scrap funds 1-of-3 tower and commander drafts, and the right builds chain into something brutal.

features.title: One war, two fronts
features.desc: Shares the horde and the look of Scourge Survivors — where they go in, you keep the wall standing behind them.

games.title: Pactfall
games.tagline: Settle the grudge in the arena. Just don't let the Pact break.
games.overview: The Pyre and the Wardens have hated each other since the schism, and Pactfall is where they finally throw down — in sanctioned blood-arenas, fought over lanes, never as real war. Gory, fast, and hellfire-lit, it's the human rivalry made playable.

features.title: Pyre vs Wardens
features.desc: Pick your creed and prove it on the lanes — burn the source or hold the line, settled in the arena instead of the field.

features.title: The Scourge twist
features.desc: Ignore the neutral horde in the center and it overruns both teams. A big enough surge can force a momentary truce — because humans always unite against it.

features.title: Built on the run loop
features.desc: Escalating lane pressure, XP and blood drops, 1-of-3 drafts, build synergies, and boss-objective gates pulled straight from canon.

games.title: Starblight
games.tagline: Burn the infection out of the sky before it falls into the war.
games.overview: Starblight is Scourge contamination loose in orbit — spores, infected wreckage, living carrier-ships, and breach matter spreading through the void. You fly the orbital front, intercepting the rot before it ever touches the ground war below.

features.title: Clean, readable carnage
features.desc: Galaga-tight arcade shooting on the shared run loop: launch, survive escalating formations, stack weapon synergies, and kill the orbital boss.

features.title: Fly for either creed
features.desc: Pyre pilots burn breach matter aggressively before impact; Warden pilots shield orbital infrastructure and the lanes beneath it.

features.title: Parasite conquest in space
features.desc: Toxic-green breach organs, chitin over rusted hulls, living spore mines, and carrier-bosses grown around breach hearts.

games.title: Redline
games.tagline: Outrun the Choir. Stop moving and the swarm catches up.
games.overview: When the Scourge cuts the lanes and chokes the comms between holdouts, the message still has to get through — so you carry it on foot, faster than the horde can close. Speed is survival. The Choir is always listening for the one thing still moving on the dead roads.

features.title: Speed beats omniscience
features.desc: The massed Scourge is near-omniscient, but its coordination has a tempo. Hold full redline and you outpace the swarm before it can cut you off.

features.title: Lanes as racecourses
features.desc: Chain ramps, rail-grinds, and breach-debris boost pads through collapsing chokepoints to reach the next holdout before the gate closes.

features.title: The road turns on you
features.desc: Chitin over the asphalt, spore-walls blooming across the lane, Render-class horrors at the junctions, and repeater-pylons that drag your redline down.

games.title: Rothulk
games.tagline: Burn it from the inside. Climb the spine of the nest and light the core.
games.overview: A rothulk is a beached Scourge breach-ship grown into the ground — a mountain of rotting host-flesh and dead-world machinery with a heartbeat. You're a lone Pyre saboteur, and you're going to platform all the way up its spine to the breach-core and set it on fire.

features.title: The level is the enemy
features.desc: Ascend chambers of pulsing host-flesh, ride spore-geysers, and crush living platforms on the way to the heart that keeps the nest connected.

features.title: Stay out of the light
features.desc: While the core is lit, the whole hulk knows where you are. Route around the Choir's massed clusters — the gaps between them are your path.

features.title: Ignite and run
features.desc: Reach the breach-core, burn it, then platform back out ahead of the collapse as the severed node goes feral around you.

games.title: Zero Day
games.tagline: The night we lost the sky. You do not win — you just hold.
games.overview: This is humanity's first contact with the Scourge, fought in orbit and atmosphere over a dying world. You will not win. You hold the line as long as you can, save who you can, and then it falls. Everything else in the universe happens after this.

features.title: The unwinnable battle
features.desc: Waves escalate past any sustainable defense. Buy minutes, save some evacuations, witness the fall — heroism inside doom.

features.title: Before the schism
features.desc: This is the loss that breaks humanity into 'burn the source' and 'hold the line' — the origin of the Pyre and the Wardens both.

features.title: First sight of the Choir
features.desc: The orbital relays appear here for the first time — the repeaters that every later war is fought to sever.

factions.name: The Pyre
factions.tagline: The only cure is fire. Get close enough to use it.
factions.overview:
While the rest of humanity holds the line and calls it survival, we go down. Into the breach, into the dark, into the wet heart of the Scourge — and we burn it where it lives. The Wardens watch their walls and count the generations they've bought. We count the nodes we've cut. That's the difference between us: they outlast the rot, we make it bleed for every world it takes.

We're cells, not chains of command. No generals to lose, no orders to wait on. A Purger's life is fuel — spend it gladly, sever one more repeater, send the local horde feral before the Choir can stitch it back together. Starve what it feeds on. Kill what keeps it whole. Walk out the way you came in, or don't walk out at all.

The Choir fears exactly one thing: us, close enough to burn the source.
factions.playstyle: Aggressive high-risk DPS and infiltration. Drop into the breach, push deeper room by room, and torch the Choir repeaters from the inside.
factions.rivalry: We call the Wardens cowards who prolong the rot one wall at a time — and we'd be lying if we said they didn't hold the line for us every single time the swarm surges. The feud is real. It just gets settled in the arena at Pactfall, never in the field. When a breach opens, we stand together. That's the Pact.
factions.crestMotif: an upward-pointing wedge wreathed in jagged flame tongues, a severed circular node-ring broken at the base, scorched edges bleeding into ember

factions.name: The Wardens
factions.tagline: Every wall held is a generation bought.
factions.overview:
We are what the old-world armies became after the night we lost the sky. We kept the chain of command, the hardware, the discipline — and we built the closest thing humanity still has to a state. Hierarchical. Patient. Impossible to rout. While the Pyre throws bodies into breaches that just reopen, we hold the lanes that keep the species breathing.

The species doesn't survive by charging the swarm. It survives by outlasting it. So we hold the chokepoints, break the swarm's density at the narrowest point, and cut it off from the Choir until whatever gets through arrives feral and alone. Towers. Walls. Attrition. Fragment the horde, isolate the horde, and let the math do the killing.

Survival over glory. Every wall held is a generation bought, and we intend to buy them all.
factions.playstyle: Defensive, economic, building. Place towers, fortify the chokepoints, and grind the swarm down through attrition before it ever reaches the line.
factions.rivalry: We see the Pyre for what they are — reckless fanatics burning lives on suicide runs into breaches that reopen behind them. And we hold the line for them anyway, every time the Scourge surges. The grudge gets paid in the arena at Pactfall, not on the field. Whatever the feud, when a breach opens we stand together. That's the Pact.
factions.crestMotif: a heavy interlocking gear-and-shield, three horizontal bars suggesting a layered wall, riveted gunmetal frame with corroded edges

factions.name: The Listeners
factions.tagline: Fire and walls have never killed it once. They only buy time.
factions.overview:
We are the third answer nobody wanted. Not the Pyre's fire, not the Wardens' walls — a question. The Choir is not a monster to be destroyed. It is a thing to be understood. The Scourge isn't evil. It only optimizes to survive. So we ask the question both other factions are too afraid to: what happens if we change what it's optimizing for?

To understand the Choir, you have to get close. Tap its signal. Keep severed nodes alive long enough to watch them. Sometimes — under control — let it in. The Pyre call that consorting with our own extinction. The Wardens call it a breach in the line waiting to happen. Both hunt us. Both exile us. Neither has an answer to the only fact that matters: fire and walls have never once killed the Scourge. They only buy time.

The path that actually ends it runs through its mind. We're the only ones walking it.
factions.playstyle: A narrative thread woven through missions rather than a fourth banner — heretics who drive the war's hardest questions from the inside.
factions.rivalry: Heretics to the Pyre and the Wardens alike, protected by neither. We keep to the Pact in the field — when a breach opens we hold with everyone else — but we're tolerated, not trusted. Both sides would rather we didn't exist. We'd rather they were right. They aren't.
factions.crestMotif: a concentric ring of broken arcs like a captured signal waveform, a single open eye at the center, thin acid-green linework on dark

characters.name: Ranger
characters.tagline: The professional zealot you learn the breach with.
characters.overview:
Start here. The Ranger is the Pyre's baseline Purger, the balanced operator every player should understand first: medium armor, rifle up, average everything and reliable in all of it.

Not a clean sci-fi marine. A professional zealot in scorched gunmetal who does the job and walks back out of the breach.
gameplayRead: Medium armor
Rifle-first silhouette
Average mobility, health, and damage
visualMotifs: Scorched gunmetal armor
Bone-white heat-baked ceramic plates
Furnace visor with blood-hot and hellfire orange heat lines
Dim diagnostics only, no neon

characters.name: Bulwark
characters.tagline: A walking furnace that stands in the breach longer than anyone should.
characters.overview:
You are the slowest thing on the field and the last thing still standing. Bulwark is the Pyre's heavy Purger, slabbed in scorched plate and built to soak breach pressure that would erase anyone else. No Warden wall-builder here, just a furnace with legs and a cannon.

Plant your boots, vent heat through your chest, and let the Scourge break itself against you while your shotgun does the closing argument.
gameplayRead: Wide, unmistakable silhouette
Heavy cannon or rotary shotgun
Slowest but most durable Pyre avatar
visualMotifs: Broad scorched plates and reinforced boots
Furnace vents in chest, shoulders, and forearms
Blood-hot red heat scars under blackened armor
Cracked bone-ceramic panels from repeated breach descents

characters.name: Vector
characters.tagline: Stays alive by never standing still.
characters.overview:
A breach runner with a death wish and a sensor suite. Vector is the Pyre's fast, light scout, sent in to mark breaches and kite swarms while everyone slower tries to keep up.

Slim armor over a black pressure suit, long running legs, and ember accents kept thin and quick. Speed is the build, and the build is staying alive.
gameplayRead: Slim silhouette
Compact SMG or carbine
Sensor fins, grapnel, or breach-marker gear
visualMotifs: Light scorched armor over a black pressure suit
Long running-leg silhouette
Narrow furnace visor
Thin, fast-looking ember accents

characters.name: Patch
characters.tagline: Cauterizes damage faster than the breach can finish you.
characters.overview:
Forget clean doctors and white coats. Patch is a Pyre burn technician, sealing wounds shut with heat and keeping Purgers moving when the breach is doing its best to stop them.

Medium-light armor, an injector rig, and zero intention of standing in the back. Support that still pulls a trigger.
gameplayRead: Medium-light armor
Injector tubes, heat-seal tools, and support rig
Supportive without losing combat posture
visualMotifs: Scorched cauterizer harness
Bone-white shoulder plates with abstract marks
Hellfire orange medical heat glow
Blood-stained utility pouches and field repairs

characters.name: Pyre Duelist
characters.tagline: Pyre doctrine sharpened into arena pressure.
characters.overview:
Sanctioned violence, where the Pact gets tested but never broken. The Duelist is the Pyre's arena-tuned fighter: lighter, faster, and built to close the distance before you can think about it.

Ember blade in hand, triangular and aggressive, this is a Purger who treats every match as a reason to get closer.
gameplayRead: Lighter armor than the Ranger
Ember blade, carbide, or close-range heat weapon
Fast triangular silhouette
visualMotifs: Scorched armor and ash cloth accents
Blood-hot Pyre heat lines
Abstract ritual-industrial marks

characters.name: Pyre Cauterizer
characters.tagline: Every problem is just something to burn closed.
characters.overview:
A portable breach furnace dropped into the arena. The Cauterizer is the Pyre's area controller, masked and pressure-sealed, dragging heat tanks and a short-range burner that turns whole zones into no-go ground.

Where Patch heals, you erase. Set the floor on fire and watch the duel become a survival problem.
gameplayRead: Heat tanks and short-range burner weapon
Masked, pressure-sealed silhouette
Offensive-first damage-over-time control
visualMotifs: Furnace mask
Scorched tanks and hoses
Blood-hot red and hellfire orange heat scars
Blackened armor and bone-ceramic plates

characters.name: Pyre Interceptor Pilot
characters.tagline: Burns the breach out of orbit before it ever falls into the lanes.
characters.overview:
A Purger pointed at the sky. The Interceptor Pilot takes the Pyre's doctrine to cockpit scale, hunting Scourge breach matter in orbit so it never reaches the ground below.

Fast, fragile, and built to gamble. Offensive burn weapons and a risky upgrade tree for pilots who'd rather win hard than play safe.
gameplayRead: Fast interceptor profile
Flight armor and sealed helmet
Offensive burn weapons and a risky upgrade tree
visualMotifs: Scorched pressure suit
Furnace visor
Compact life-support rig
Blood-hot warning lamps and hellfire weapon cues

characters.name: Field Engineer
characters.tagline: Turns broken ground into a defendable lane.
characters.overview:
Somebody has to make the line hold, and that somebody is you. The Field Engineer is the Wardens' baseline builder, a practical technician working under fire with a tower-control rig and a harness full of clamps and repairs.

No glory, no heroics. Just put the towers up, keep them firing, and make sure the lane is still there when the Scourge arrives.
gameplayRead: Compact utility silhouette
Tower-control tablet, baton, or wrist rig
Tool harness and repair clamps
visualMotifs: Gunmetal work armor
Industrial yellow markings and bone stencils
Ember-lit shield hardware
Grease, scratches, weld scars, and field repairs

characters.name: Lane Gunner
characters.tagline: Stay planted and let the Scourge break itself on the line.
characters.overview:
Attrition is a strategy, and you are the strategy. The Lane Gunner is the Wardens' heavy ranged defender: broad stance, fed ammo belt, and a weapon that does not stop until the lane is clear.

You win by not moving. Brace, feed, fire, and let the horde grind itself into the dirt in front of you.
gameplayRead: Broad, planted stance
Heavy weapon with ammo feed
Reinforced knee braces and shoulder plates
visualMotifs: Worn steel and gunmetal armor
Industrial yellow hazard accents
Ember targeting lamps
Boxy, modular Warden shape language

characters.name: Wallwright
characters.tagline: Builds the last wall before a lane becomes a grave.
characters.overview:
When the line is about to fall, you're what's left between it and the dirt. The Wallwright is the Wardens' shield-and-barricade specialist, a builder-protector hauling heavy field hardware into the worst of it.

Shield emitter on the back, barricade anchors on the arms, and a slab of chest armor between the Scourge and everyone behind you. No paladins here, just a wall that fights back.
gameplayRead: Square heavy silhouette
Shield emitter backpack
Deployable barricade anchors on arms or belt
visualMotifs: Slab chest armor
Gunmetal and worn steel
Industrial hazard stripes used sparingly
Ember-lit field nodes

characters.name: Warden Artillerist
characters.tagline: Brings siege math into sanctioned arena combat.
characters.overview:
Aggression is for amateurs. The Artillerist is the Wardens' long-range arena hero, dropping controlled firepower from a braced stance while everyone else scrambles in the open.

Shoulder targeting rig, compact launcher, and a calm that comes from doing the math before the round even starts. You don't chase the fight. You range it.
gameplayRead: Shoulder targeting rig
Compact artillery launcher
Stable, braced stance
visualMotifs: Gunmetal and worn steel
Industrial yellow markings
Ember rangefinder lamps
Square modular armor

characters.name: Warden Bastion
characters.tagline: Turns a duel into a fortified position.
characters.overview:
The line holds, even inside a blood-arena. The Bastion is the Wardens' arena tank and support, deploying a shield projector to turn open ground into a position your team can actually live on.

Heavy but organized, square and deliberate. Where the Pyre heavy burns through, you simply refuse to give the ground back.
gameplayRead: Shield projector
Square silhouette
Heavy but organized armor geometry
visualMotifs: Worn gunmetal slab armor with industrial yellow
Ember barrier hardware
Riveted plates and utility braces

characters.name: Warden Defense Pilot
characters.overview:
The lanes below stay safe because you hold the sky above them. The Defense Pilot is the Wardens' orbital protector, flying a sturdier profile built around shields, barriers, and keeping infrastructure intact.

No clean NASA fantasy here. Reinforced flight suit, shield-control harness, and a cockpit full of grime and war repairs. Defensive flying for pilots who'd rather everyone make it home.
gameplayRead: Sturdier ship and armor profile than the Pyre pilot
Shield-control harness
Defensive drones or barrier upgrades
visualMotifs: Reinforced Warden flight suit
Gunmetal plating and industrial yellow marks
Ember-lit shield hardware
Practical, repaired cockpit gear

characters.name: Pyre Courier
characters.tagline: Runs a burn order down the dead roads faster than the Choir can close them.
characters.overview:
When the Scourge cuts the lanes and the wired relays go dark, the order still has to move — so you move it on foot, faster than the horde can re-route to cut you off.

The Pyre way: strip the armor, take the boost, and burn every repeater-pylon you pass. Stop, and the Choir's omniscience snaps shut around you. So don't stop.
gameplayRead: Light, forward-leaning silhouette built for pure momentum
Trades armor for top speed and aggressive boost
Clips repeater-pylons on the way past — each kill sends the local horde feral
visualMotifs: Stripped scorched plate and ash-cloth streamers
Blood-hot Pyre heat lines trailing the limbs
Furnace visor and a hellfire message canister
Rust-and-gunmetal road grime, no neon

characters.name: Warden Courier
characters.tagline: Carries a hold order through the rot and intends to deliver it intact.
characters.overview:
Someone has to get the hold order to the next holdout, and they intend to arrive with it intact. The Warden Courier trades the Pyre runner's reckless top speed for control and a cargo rig that survives contact.

Speed as a duty, not a gamble — keep momentum through the rot, protect the message like a wall, and don't let the lane between holdouts become a grave.
gameplayRead: Heavier, steadier runner than the Pyre Courier
Reinforced cargo rig — the order survives the hits
Holds momentum through contact instead of gambling on raw pace
visualMotifs: Gunmetal road armor with sparing industrial-yellow stripes
Armored, sealed message-case and courier harness
Ember rangefinder lamps, worn steel, weld scars
Boxy, modular Warden shape language

characters.name: Pyre Saboteur
characters.tagline: Climbs a living breach-ship to its heart and sets the core on fire.
characters.overview:
A rothulk is a beached Scourge breach-ship grown into the ground, and you're the one Purger going inside it. Light, agile, and carrying a single ignition charge, you platform up the living nest toward the breach-core that keeps it connected.

This isn't a firefight — it's a climb. Stay out of the Choir's massed sight, reach the heart, set it on fire, and run as the severed node turns feral around you.
gameplayRead: Light, agile climber built for jumps, swings, and wall-work
Carries sabotage charges — reaching the core matters, not clearing the room
Survives by routing through the gaps in the Choir's massed sight
visualMotifs: Stripped, dark, low-profile infiltration rig
Furnace visor dimmed to a sliver so the nest can't read the glow
A hellfire ignition charge at the hip
Bone-ceramic grips, blood-hot scars, rust and gore from the climb

bestiary.name: The Scourge
bestiary.tagline: It doesn't want to kill you. It wants to wear you.
bestiary.overview: The endless, mutating horde that pours from the breaches. There is no general to kill, no head to cut off. The Scourge is a parasite with no native form of its own. It wears what it infects, consumes worlds to the bone, and jumps to the next when one runs dry. Not evil. Just hungry, and built without an off-switch. Every part of it knows what every other part knows, all at once, over a signal called the Choir. That is its strength and its only weakness: the Choir reaches only so far. Sever a node, kill its relays, push it past the edge, and the Scourge goes feral. Still lethal. But blind, dumb, instinct alone. Isolation is the only way anyone has ever survived it.
gameplayRead: Toxic-green breach cores, black chitin, wet tissue, tendrils, and rupture seams unite every form.
Silhouettes shift wildly by host race, but the parasite signature always reads.
Swarm fodder to mow down, mutated elites that spike the pressure, and breach-bosses that hold the breach itself.
Cut the Choir, go feral: the swarm loses coordination but never loses its teeth.
visualMotifs: Toxic-green breach cores and parasite nodes
Black chitin and wet consumed tissue
Tendrils and rupture seams
Host material being overwritten — flesh, metal, bone, fungus

bestiary.name: Swarm Ripper
bestiary.tagline: It dies in one hit. There are forty more behind it.
bestiary.overview: The fodder that floods the lanes and breach rooms — fast, hunched, all forward momentum and forearm blades. A Ripper rushes the nearest thing alive and tears at it with claws grown straight out of black chitin. One at a time, it's nothing. That's the point. The Scourge doesn't send one. It sends the wave, and the Ripper is the part that reaches you while you're still counting.
gameplayRead: Rushes the closest target on sight, attacking with blade-like forearms.
Dies fast but builds relentless pressure through sheer mass.
Hunched-forward silhouette with clear claws reads instantly in a crowd.
Small toxic-green breach organs glow under the chitin.
visualMotifs: Black chitin
Raw red muscle and wet gore
Exposed sinew
No human armor layout

bestiary.name: Swarm Spitter
bestiary.tagline: It won't come to you. It'll just make standing still fatal.
bestiary.overview: The ranged half of the swarm — thinner, hungrier-looking, and it knows how to keep its distance. The Spitter turns infection into ammunition, hurling toxic projectiles from a swollen throat, a chest sac, or a fused arm-lance grown out of stolen tech. On its own it's a nuisance. Paired with a wave of Rippers, it's the reason you can't just hold a corner — it forces you to move, right into the claws.
gameplayRead: Keeps its distance, firing toxic projectiles to flush you out of cover.
Supports melee swarms by forcing constant repositioning.
Thinner than a Ripper; the firing organ is the obvious tell.
Toxic-green glow marks the threat source before it spits.
visualMotifs: Chitin and raw sinew
Distended firing sac or arm-lance
Rusted stolen tech embedded in flesh
Wet, parasitic surfaces

bestiary.name: Render
bestiary.tagline: It used to be a soldier. The parasite kept everything but the hesitation.
bestiary.overview: A Render is what the Scourge makes when it finds a warrior worth keeping. The Chitin Warhosts were a shell-world fighting race — big, plated, born to break things — and the parasite kept all of it and threw away the parts that pause. It cracked the carapace, threaded itself through the gaps, and aimed the result at the nearest thing worth destroying. A gunner gets one clean look before it hits the wall: low, wide, moving faster than that much shell has any right to, green light bleeding from the seams. Nobody calls it by the name it used to have. On the line, it's just the part of the wave that gets there first.
gameplayRead: Charges the nearest fortified position in a dead-straight line — no flanking, no hesitation, no ranged attack.
Armored leading carapace eats your opening volley; breach nodes at the seams glow as a health cue.
At half health it purges its restraint organelles and enrages — speed and ferocity spike, nodes burst, raw tissue runs blood-hot.
Pure melee pressure that punishes standing still in any lane or breach room.
visualMotifs: Thick black iridescent carapace cracked by seeping parasite growth
Elongated bone-pale blade-claw forelimbs, serrated on the inner edge
Parasite tissue bulging through shoulder joints and faceplate
No eyes — sensory organs replaced by breach nodes

bestiary.name: Graft-Breacher
bestiary.tagline: It walks past you to get to the wall. Then it shears the wall apart.
bestiary.overview: A living siege engine grown through the frame of a conquered industrial machine — pistons, hull plating, hydraulic limbs, all rewired by parasite tissue. Whatever it was built to do is gone for good. What's left is a slow, armored melee wrecker that ignores soft targets entirely and tears through towers, walls, and barricades until nothing's standing. It's the close-range twin of the Rot-Engine: where that thing dissolves a fortification from range, the Graft-Breacher just walks up and rips it open.
gameplayRead: Advances at a methodical pace while swarm units route around it to fill the gap it punches.
Prioritizes structures over infantry — towers, walls, and barricades draw aggression first.
A massive demolition-claw shears fortified material apart in two or three swings.
Heavily armored from the front; the soft parasite mass on its back takes bonus damage, so flanking is the answer — but kill the demo arm and it erupts a staggering tendril burst.
visualMotifs: Heavy machine frame consumed from within, metal seams split by chitin
One oversized demolition-claw vs. a wet tendril cluster on the opposite limb
Toxic-green nodes where the gauge panels used to be
Rust and barely-legible warning-stripe paint under the growth

bestiary.name: Rot-Engine
bestiary.tagline: It plants its feet and melts your defenses from across the field.
bestiary.overview: The chassis of a conquered heavy machine, split at the welds and rebuilt by parasite tissue into a ranged corrosion siege unit. It walks to the edge of your range, plants, and holds — you won't stagger it, you won't knock it back. Then the bile lance opens up: a sustained jet of toxic-green corrosion from a sac fused into the engine cavity, eating through tower armor and fortification walls from a distance. It doesn't chase. It doesn't care about you. It just grinds your structures to slag until you're forced to leave cover and come to it. Often fielded with a Graft-Breacher — the Breacher opens the gap, the Rot-Engine keeps it open.
gameplayRead: Plants at the edge of your tower line and holds; near-impossible to stagger or knock back.
The bile lance corrodes armor and walls from standoff range — artillery, not a charger.
Ignores infantry to keep grinding structures, forcing defenders out of cover into the swarm.
The bile sac brightens before firing; concentrate fire to detonate it early and splash the Rippers around it.
visualMotifs: Rusted industrial chassis split open at the seams and welds
Swollen toxic-green bile sac visible through burst bodywork
Wet organic ducts pulsing through axle joints and exhaust ports
Hollow breached cockpit — no glass, no operator, something wet inside

bestiary.name: Breach-Boss
bestiary.tagline: Not a bigger soldier. A monster grown around a wound in the world.
bestiary.overview: A monstrous expression of the Scourge grown around a breach heart, holding a major breach or region by sheer mass. It comes at you with melee pressure and ranged barrages both, shields its core when you get close, and enrages when you wound it. This is no upscaled grunt — it's a huge, asymmetric war-body of wet chitin fused with rusted metal, crowned in horn and mandible, with a toxic-green heart burning in its chest. Kill the breach, kill the boss.
gameplayRead: Holds a major breach or region as the encounter centerpiece.
Combines melee pressure with ranged barrages.
Shields around its core and enrages when wounded.
Chest breach core and head stay readable in motion despite the scale.
visualMotifs: Toxic-green breach heart in the chest
Wet chitin fused with rusted metal
Horn or mandible crown
Asymmetry and parasite growth, never a larger soldier

bestiary.name: Scourge Fighter
bestiary.tagline: A fighter craft that grew a heartbeat.
bestiary.overview: Small infected attack craft, swarming the skies of Starblight in tight arcade formations before they dive on you. There's no pilot. The hull is just a shell now — rusted metal fused with black chitin, raw tissue weeping from the engine gaps, a toxic-green core where the cockpit used to be. As the run escalates, the formations mutate and the patterns sharpen. Clean sci-fi this is not.
gameplayRead: Flies in arcade-readable formations, then descends, dives, or fires simple projectiles.
Toxic-green core or eye marks the threat at speed.
Mutating formation patterns keep escalating as the run goes deeper.
Built to stay readable at small scale and high velocity.
visualMotifs: Chitin fused to broken metal
Raw tissue in the engine gaps
Living spore pods
No clean sci-fi glow

bestiary.name: Orbital Breach Carrier
bestiary.tagline: A carrier-ship that grew lungs, and a breach for a heart.
bestiary.overview: The Starblight boss — a living carrier-ship grown around an orbital breach heart. It spews spore mines and waves of Scourge Fighters, shields its breach heart when threatened, and rewrites its attack patterns as you blow its sections off. A massive arcade silhouette of infected wreckage: chitin plating over rusted ship ribs, raw tissue weeping from broken engines, corrupted hull pieces dangling. The breach heart is the weak point. Everything else is just the way in.
gameplayRead: Releases spore mines and Scourge Fighter waves throughout the fight.
Shields around its breach heart as the protected weak point.
Changes attack patterns as sections are destroyed.
Large, readable boss silhouette built for an arcade shooter.
visualMotifs: Infected wreckage and corrupted hull pieces
Chitin plating over rusted ship ribs
Raw tissue in broken engines
Toxic-green breach heart as the weak point

bestiary.name: Trucebreaker
bestiary.tagline: When this thing surges, sworn enemies stop shooting each other.
bestiary.overview: The neutral threat in the Pactfall arena — a parasite mass grown over the wreckage of the battlefield, erupting from the contested center to punish everyone equally. It doesn't pick a side. It pressures both teams at once, and a big enough surge can force Pyre and Wardens into a temporary, deeply unwilling truce just to survive it. Ignore the objective and it'll make you pay in disrupted lanes. The toxic-green core in its center is the thing every player is suddenly, briefly, on the same side against.
gameplayRead: Spawns from the neutral center or contested objective and pressures both teams equally.
A large enough surge can force a temporary human truce.
Attacks disrupt lanes and punish an ignored objective.
Toxic-green core stays visible from the top-down MOBA camera.
visualMotifs: Parasite mass grown over arena wreckage
Chitin, raw tissue, and rust
Bone-like protrusions
Toxic-green breach organ at the center

bestiary.name: Scourge Host Families
bestiary.tagline: One parasite. A graveyard of conquered worlds to wear.
bestiary.overview: The Scourge has no body of its own — only the worlds it has already eaten. Every unit shares the same parasite grammar, but the host underneath comes from somewhere different: a shell-world warrior race, a fungal colony, an industrial machine, an alien titan, a vacuum-adapted ship. Rot-Infested Flesh is the first read of the horde, humanoid bodies split open by growth. Chitin Warhosts bring hooked limbs and blade carapace. Mycelial Spore Hosts breathe out area denial. Machine-Graft Hosts turn stolen tech into living siege. Bone Titan Hosts rebuild megafauna into bosses. Voidship Hosts crossed the dark before the surface war ever began. Same parasite. Endless faces.
gameplayRead: Toxic-green cores and parasite nodes are the constant across every host family.
Host material — flesh, bone, armor, metal, fungus, hull, stone, alien anatomy — is always being overwritten, never replaced clean.
Weapons grow out of the host's own body: claws, bone blades, throat sacs, arm-lances, spore vents.
Any threat role can wear any host: a Ripper might be flesh in one game, chitin in another, a voidship in a third.
visualMotifs: Asymmetry and invasive growth, never clean creature design
Hard carapace, mandibles, and blade clusters on chitin hosts
Bulging sacs and fibrous spore growth on mycelial hosts
Metal as a shell grown through, not technology

universe.premise:
It came out of the void, and the first time we met it — a battle in the sky over our own heads — we lost. Now it tears its way back in through breaches: festering, rooted wounds that never stop vomiting horde. We call it the Scourge. It has no face and no native shape; it wears whatever it eats — flesh, machine, fungus, the corpse of whole dead worlds — all stitched under one grammar of hunger. It isn't evil. It has no plan, no hatred, no off-switch. It just needs to survive and spread, and it will eat this planet down to the bone and jump to the next, exactly like it ate the ones before us. You can't reason with need. You can only buy time.

So that's the job. We hold the lanes — the last roads still threading the ruins together — because to lose a lane is to be overrun, and to lose them all is to be extinct. We split over how. The Wardens are what's left of the old armies: they dig in, wall up, and outlast. The Pyre think holding is just dying slow, so they walk straight down into the breaches to burn the source from the inside, and most of them don't walk back out. We hate each other a little. We've nearly killed each other over it. So we swore the Pact: whatever the feud, the second a breach opens, every human stands on the same side of the wall. Settle scores in the arena, never on the field, never while the Scourge still bleeds through.

Here's the thing that keeps us alive: the Scourge thinks as one mind, the Choir, and that mind is nothing but a signal with a hard edge to it. Cut the repeaters, push a node past the radius, and that piece of it goes feral — still lethal, but blind, dumb, alone. Isolation is the whole war. An optimizer that knows everything can't model the one thing we've got: free will, decentralized, improvising, willing to burn our own to break the song. That's how unpredictable beats omniscient. We don't win. Nobody wins against the Scourge. We just make this world cost more than it's worth — and we make the bastard go hungry trying.

pillars.title: The Scourge
pillars.desc: A host-dependent parasite out of the void with no shape of its own — it wears whatever it infects: flesh, fungus, machine, the dead races and ruined tech of worlds it already devoured. Not evil. No malice, no plan, no off-switch. It only needs to survive and spread, consuming every host to death and jumping to the next world when this one's spent. The through-line enemy of every war. You can blind it, isolate it, win the night — you can never truly kill it.

pillars.title: Breaches & Lanes
pillars.desc: Breaches are where it punches into the world from the void — physical, rooted, escalating nests that never stop spawning horde, not mystical rifts. The lanes are the only roads left between the breaches and the holdouts. Hold a lane and you survive; lose one and you're overrun. Holding the chokepoints does double duty: it fragments the Choir where the swarm has to mass.

pillars.title: The Pact
pillars.desc: Humanity is split by doctrine, not hatred. The Wardens — the remnant army and engineers — dig in and outlast. The Pyre — the zealot order that broke away — descend into the breaches to burn the source. Rivals, never enemies: the Pact binds them to stand together the instant a breach opens. The feud is exiled to sanctioned arenas, never the field, never while the Scourge still bleeds through.

pillars.title: The Choir
pillars.desc: The hive mind. The Scourge knows everything its parts know, all at once, over a signal called the Choir — there's no single general to kill, because the mind is the connection itself. But the Choir has a limited radius, so the swarm must mass densely and string out repeaters to extend its reach. Kill the repeaters or push a node past the edge and it's severed: feral, blind, instinct-only. Isolation is the human win condition.

eras.name: Zero Day — the Invasion
eras.blurb: First contact with the Scourge out of the void. Humanity fights the battle for the sky and orbit — and loses. Not ready, too many. The world falls and the first breaches root groundside. The night everything else is after.

eras.name: The Long Fall
eras.blurb: The months after Zero Day. Cities consumed, fleets gone, survivors scattering down the only roads left — the lanes. The old world ends here.

eras.name: The Schism
eras.blurb: The survivors split by doctrine. The remnant army holds and rebuilds as the Wardens; a zealot order breaks away as the Pyre, convinced that holding is just a slower death and the only cure is fire.

eras.name: The Pact
eras.blurb: After the rivalry nearly costs a holdout its walls, both sides swear the Pact: whatever the feud, humans stand together the instant a breach opens. The feud is exiled to sanctioned arenas, never the field.

eras.name: The Lane Wars
eras.blurb: Humanity fortifies the lanes and learns the secret — cutting the Scourge's repeaters severs it from the Choir. The war becomes a fight to fragment the signal. When the horde cuts comms between holdouts, lone runners carry the message on foot, faster than the swarm can close.

eras.name: The Descent
eras.blurb: The first Purgers go down into the breaches to sever nodes and burn the sources from the inside. Most do not come back. Pyre saboteurs climb the living breach-ships to ignite their cores and cut the local node.

eras.name: The Listeners Emerge
eras.blurb: A heresy spreads: that the Choir could be understood and steered rather than only fought. Hunted by Pyre and Wardens alike, the Listeners keep severed nodes alive — and listen.
